blueprint: Two strokes — a person walking, seen in profile. The sweeping left stroke is the torso and leading leg; the right is the trailing leg. Compressed to 亻 on the left of person-related characters.
formula: left leg 丿 + right leg ㇏ = 人
cultural_note: In oracle-bone script, 人 depicted a person walking in profile — the defining mark of humanity in ancient pictographs. The front-facing form, arms spread wide, became 大.
mnemonic: Two legs walking — a person seen from the side. The strokes ARE the legs.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><circle cx='48' cy='20' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#5a4f44' stroke-width='2.5'/><line x1='48' y1='28' x2='50' y2='50' stroke='#5a4f44' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='32' y2='82' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='66' y2='82' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='30' cy='72' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='30' y='72' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='66' cy='70' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='66' y='70' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text></svg>

blueprint: A central downward current flanked by two droplets — water seen in cross-section. Its contracted radical 氵 (three drops) marks over 1,000 water-related characters.
formula: central current 亅 + droplets 丶丶 = 水
cultural_note: 水 contracts to 氵 on the left of compounds — "the three drops" — giving its meaning to 河 (river), 海 (sea), 泳 (swim), 洗 (wash), and hundreds more.
mnemonic: A river in cross-section — the central current with droplets breaking off each side.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><line x1='50' y1='12' x2='50' y2='88' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M50,28 Q65,32 63,46' fill='none' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='2.8' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M50,42 Q35,38 37,54' fill='none' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M50,58 Q65,62 63,74' fill='none' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='70' cy='36' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='70' y='36' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='58' cy='18' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='58' y='18' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='27' cy='48' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='27' y='48' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='73' cy='68' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='73' y='68' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>

blueprint: Two sparks above a flame body shaped like 人 — fire caught mid-leap. Contracted to 灬 (four dots) beneath characters it heats: 热 rè hot, 蒸 zhēng steam.
formula: sparks 丶丶 + flame 人 = 火
cultural_note: 火车 "fire vehicle" = train. When steam locomotives arrived in 19th-century China, the language reached for its oldest word for unstoppable, visible power.
mnemonic: A fire leaping — sparks at the top, the flame's legs spreading below.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><path d='M50,82 Q56,65 50,28' fill='none' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M46,65 Q28,54 31,74' fill='none' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='2.8' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M54,65 Q72,54 69,74' fill='none' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='4' fill='#1a2744'/><circle cx='62' cy='54' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='62' y='54' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='22' cy='62' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='22' y='62' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='78' cy='62' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='78' y='62' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='62' cy='20' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='62' y='20' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>

blueprint: Three peaks rising from a single baseline — one of the least-changed characters from oracle bone to today. 山 is its own radical, appearing in characters for peaks, ridges, and rugged terrain.
formula: left peak + tallest peak + right peak = 山
cultural_note: 山水 — "mountains and waters" — names the genre of landscape painting that dominated Chinese classical art for a millennium. Two characters carry an entire artistic tradition.
mnemonic: A mountain range — three peaks sharing one base.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><line x1='18' y1='80' x2='82' y2='80' stroke='#5a4f44' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='80' x2='50' y2='18' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='28' y1='80' x2='28' y2='46' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='72' y1='80' x2='72' y2='46' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='60' cy='25' r='7' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='60' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='17' cy='56' r='7' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='17' y='56' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='83' cy='56' r='7' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='83' y='56' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text></svg>

blueprint: A cross-section of a standing tree: the horizontal stroke is the branches, the vertical is the trunk, and the two lower strokes are the roots. 木 appears in nearly every character related to wood or plants.
formula: branches 一 + trunk 丨 + roots 丿㇏ = 木
cultural_note: 木 loses its roots and gains a dot to become 本 (běn) — origin, basis, root. The dot marks where the root is. Adding a horizontal stroke at the top gives 末 (mò) — tip, end.
mnemonic: A standing tree — trunk, spreading branch, and roots fanning below.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><line x1='50' y1='12' x2='50' y2='88' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='22' y1='32' x2='78' y2='32' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='75' x2='28' y2='92' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='75' x2='72' y2='92' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='57' cy='18' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='57' y='18' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='84' cy='32' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='84' y='32' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='22' cy='88' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='22' y='88' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='78' cy='88' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='78' y='88' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>

blueprint: A circle with a dot at centre — the sun's disc and its blinding core. Squared off in clerical script. The central line distinguishes 日 (sun) from 口 (mouth), which is just an open box.
formula: disc ⼝ + core 一 = 日
cultural_note: 日本 (Rìběn) — Japan — means "origin of the sun", named from China's eastern perspective. The kanji 日本 is still used in Japanese today.
mnemonic: The sun — a bright disc with a gleam of light at its very centre.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><rect x='28' y='18' width='44' height='64' rx='2' fill='none' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='28' y1='50' x2='72' y2='50' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='78' cy='25' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='78' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='78' cy='57' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='78' y='57' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>

blueprint: A crescent laid on its back, with two strokes inside indicating the moon's phases. 月 also doubles as the 'flesh/body' radical 肉 in many anatomical characters — same shape, different meaning.
formula: crescent ⺆ + phases 二 = 月
cultural_note: 月饼 (yuèbǐng) — mooncakes — are eaten during the Mid-Autumn Festival (中秋节) when the moon is at its fullest. The round cake mirrors the full moon.
mnemonic: A crescent moon laid on its back — the arc and two faint lines of its phases.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><path d='M48,12 Q20,35 20,50 Q20,65 48,88' fill='none' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M48,12 Q68,30 66,50 Q64,70 48,88' fill='none' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='32' y1='40' x2='60' y2='40' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' opacity='0.4'/><line x1='32' y1='60' x2='60' y2='60' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' opacity='0.4'/><circle cx='22' cy='25' r='7' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='22' y='25' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='76' cy='28' r='7' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='76' y='28' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='72' cy='40' r='7' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='72' y='40' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='72' cy='60' r='7' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='72' y='60' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='9' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>

blueprint: A person (人) standing upright with arms flung wide — indicating size by embodying it. The oracle-bone form is explicitly a front-facing figure, arms outstretched.
formula: person 人 + arms spread wide = 大
cultural_note: 大 at the top of 天 (tiān — sky/heaven) literally shows a person beneath the sky. The dot/stroke above the head indicates what is beyond the largest thing — heaven itself.
mnemonic: A person standing with arms wide — showing how big they are.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><circle cx='50' cy='16' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#5a4f44' stroke-width='2.5'/><line x1='50' y1='24' x2='50' y2='72' stroke='#5a4f44' stroke-width='2.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='12' y1='46' x2='88' y2='46' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='72' x2='28' y2='92' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><line x1='50' y1='72' x2='72' y2='92' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='60' y='30' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='88' cy='38' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='88' y='38' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='24' cy='84' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='24' y='84' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='76' cy='84' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='76' y='84' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>
svg_caption: Arms spread as wide as possible

blueprint: Three scattered dots — things dispersed and diminished. The central hook anchors what little remains; the outer dots drift apart. An antonym of 大 built entirely from absence.
formula: central mark 亅 + two dots 丶丶 = 小
cultural_note: 小心 (xiǎoxīn) — be careful — literally means "small heart." Caution requires a mind that notices small things. This compound appears on countless Chinese warning signs.
mnemonic: Three small scattered dots — things broken into pieces, made small.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><line x1='50' y1='38' x2='50' y2='65' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M50,65 Q50,72 46,74' fill='none' stroke='#c9a960' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M32,48 L28,56' stroke='#b83a25' stroke-width='3.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><path d='M68,48 L72,56' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='3.5' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='60' cy='44' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='60' y='44' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='20' cy='50' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='20' y='50' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='80' y='50' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text></svg>

blueprint: Oracle bone script drew the actual heart: a curved body with three blood vessels branching off. The hook is the heart itself; the dots are the aortic branches. As a radical (忄) it marks emotional states.
formula: heart body ⺄ + vessels 丶丶丶 = 心
cultural_note: In Chinese medicine and philosophy, 心 is the seat of consciousness — not just emotion but thought, intention, and moral will. The mind-heart distinction that shapes Western thought does not apply.
mnemonic: A heart with three blood vessels — an ancient anatomical drawing.
svg_overlay: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><path d='M20,70 Q35,45 50,58 Q65,45 80,70' fill='none' stroke='#1a2744' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/><circle cx='30' cy='48' r='5' fill='#c9a960'/><circle cx='50' cy='40' r='5' fill='#b83a25'/><circle cx='70' cy='48' r='5' fill='#1a2744'/><circle cx='18' cy='42' r='6.5' fill='#c9a960'/><text x='18' y='42' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>1</text><circle cx='50' cy='28' r='6.5' fill='#b83a25'/><text x='50' y='28' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>2</text><circle cx='82' cy='42' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='82' y='42' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>3</text><circle cx='50' cy='82' r='6.5' fill='#1a2744'/><text x='50' y='82' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='8' font-weight='bold' fill='white' text-anchor='middle' dominant-baseline='central'>4</text></svg>
